title: Ridsdalea merrillii
taxonomy: Ridsdalea merrillii is classified within the family Rutaceae and the order Sapindales. This plant is a species of flowering plant native to the Philippines. It belongs to the genus Ridsdalea, which is named after the British botanist Thomas Gordon Hartley Ridsdale. The Rutaceae family consists of various plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. Further classification of Ridsdalea merrillii is based on its unique characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Ridsdalea merrillii, also known as the "Ridsdalea," is a type of plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
